title: L001: Add Chaos Mesh FluxCD deployment infra
summary: Clean infrastructure feature that adds Chaos Mesh to the FluxCD deployment pipeline. All 8 affected files (2 new, 6 modified) follow mandatory patterns exactly. HelmRepository matches sources/openbao.yaml structure, HelmRelease follows fluxcd-helmrelease-common-installupgrade-settings pattern, namespace follows two-phase-kustomization-for-crd-dependent-resources pattern, kind overlay follows existing strategic merge patch convention, deploy-infra.sh uses existing wait_for_helmreleases helper, Chainsaw E2E test follows JMESPath assertion pattern, and docs are updated with all required sections. No scope creep, no security concerns, no pattern violations.

tests_checklist.item: E2E test exists for Chaos Mesh controller-manager Deployment (availableReplicas > 0)
E2E test exists for Chaos Mesh chaos-daemon DaemonSet (numberReady >= 1)
Step comment header follows '# ── Step N: Description ──' convention
Covers list in file header updated to include Chaos Mesh

code_quality_checklist.item: HelmRepository matches sources/openbao.yaml format (no comments beyond header, --- separator)
HelmRelease comment block matches releases/openbao.yaml format (description, namespace, dependency, architecture ref, feature ID)
Kind overlay patch follows existing strategic merge patch structure (openbao, memcached-operator, keystone-operator)
No dead code or unused resources

security_checklist.item: No shell injection vectors in deploy-infra.sh modification

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible — minimal changes to add one operator
No scope creep — only chaos-mesh related files changed
Kind overlay correctly configures containerd runtime for kind networking
Dashboard disabled in kind (unnecessary in CI)
Resource requests reduced for single-node clusters

fail_fast_checklist.item: chaos-mesh added to wait_for_helmreleases in deploy-infra.sh — deployment waits for readiness before proceeding
E2E test asserts Chaos Mesh health before chaos tests can run

defensive_checklist.item: install.createNamespace: true as safety net for FluxCD reconciliation
Explicit Namespace resource in namespaces.yaml for kubectl apply -k path

suggested_improvements: The docs resource counts (12 files / 17 resources, 6 namespaces / 6 HelmRepositories / 5 HelmReleases) are inaccurate when compared to the actual kustomization.yaml which lists 8 namespaces (monitoring-system and openbao-system missing from docs table), 7 HelmRepositories (prometheus-community missing), and 9 HelmReleases (prometheus-operator-crds, mariadb-operator-crds, openbao, keystone-operator missing). This is a pre-existing discrepancy not introduced by this PR — fixing it would be a separate docs maintenance task.

The docs directory layout tree in the sources/ and releases/ sections omits several files present in the actual kustomization (prometheus-community.yaml, prometheus-operator-crds.yaml, mariadb-operator-crds.yaml, openbao.yaml, keystone-operator.yaml). Pre-existing — not in scope for this feature.
The cross-reference table note 'The openbao HelmRepository has no corresponding HelmRelease yet' (line 229) is stale — releases/openbao.yaml exists in the kustomization. Pre-existing stale doc entry.
next_steps: Merge this feature — all requirements are met and no blocking/major issues found.
Consider a separate docs maintenance PR to correct the pre-existing resource count and directory layout discrepancies in infrastructure-manifests.md.
